bugs.title: fullContent duplicated when companion gate rejects tool call
bugs.description: When the AI calls create_itinerary_items in companion mode and the companion gate returns 'not_needed', the tool loop re-generates the response. The fullContent field in the messageComplete event contains the original partial response concatenated with the re-generated response, resulting in duplicated text shown to the user.
bugs.steps_to_reproduce: 1. Create a trip with status ACTIVE. 2. Send a message in CHAT_MODE_COMPANION like 'just landed at BKK, what do I do first?'. 3. If the AI attempts create_itinerary_items and gets rejected, the fullContent will contain the response text twice.
bugs.expected: fullContent should contain only the final, clean response text without duplication
bugs.actual: fullContent contains the pre-tool-call text + a space + the complete re-generated response, effectively doubling the content. Message 1 had 224 words (should have been ~120). Message 5 had the entire flower market response duplicated.
bugs.api_command: buf curl --schema . --protocol connect --http2-prior-knowledge --header 'Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN' --header 'Origin: $ORIGIN' -d '{"content":"just landed at BKK, what do I do first?","mode":"CHAT_MODE_COMPANION","trip_id":"TRIP_ID"}' http://$HOST/toqui.v1.ChatService/SendMessage

ux_issues.description: Message 5 ('recommend something fun for tonight') only suggested one option (flower market). For a 26-year-old solo traveler asking about evening fun in Bangkok, multiple options would be more helpful (e.g., night markets, rooftop bars, Muay Thai, night food tours). The single-option response feels limiting.
ux_issues.suggestion: Consider prompting the AI to provide 2-3 varied evening options when asked for nightlife/evening recommendations, especially for younger solo travelers.

ux_issues.description: Message 2: The AI mentioned Jay Fai twice, then awkwardly corrected itself ('Raan Jay Fai: Actually, I just mentioned Jay Fai'). This feels unpolished and wastes the user's reading time.
ux_issues.suggestion: Minor quality issue in response generation; the AI should deduplicate its own recommendations.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: AI attempted unsolicited create_itinerary_items in companion mode on 2 out of 5 messages (messages 1 and 5). The companion gate correctly blocked these, but the AI should learn from the first rejection to not attempt it again in the same session.
ai_behavior_issues.context: Message 1 ('just landed at BKK') and Message 5 ('recommend something fun for tonight') both triggered unsolicited create_itinerary_items calls that were rejected by the companion gate with 'not_needed' status.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: Message 3 ('I think I'm lost, I'm near a big gold temple') did not mention Wat Saket (the Golden Mount), which is actually the most likely 'big gold temple' near Khao San Road. The AI guessed Grand Palace/Wat Phra Kaew and Wat Pho, which are reasonable but Wat Saket is closer to Khao San and more commonly described as a 'big gold temple'.
ai_behavior_issues.context: The persona specification suggested Wat Saket or Grand Palace area as the expected guess. The AI's guesses were reasonable but missed the most geographically likely candidate.

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: Companion mode worked well overall. Responses were generally concise and actionable with Thailand-specific advice. The companion gate correctly prevented unsolicited itinerary creation and allowed it when explicitly requested. The main issues are: (1) a P1 content duplication bug where fullContent doubles when the companion gate rejects a tool call, and (2) the AI still attempts create_itinerary_items in companion mode despite being in a reactive context. Food recommendations were concrete with specific dish names and prices. Cultural advice (tipping) was accurate and Thailand-specific. Geographic reasoning was decent but missed Wat Saket as the most likely 'big gold temple' near Khao San.
